blocks.title: Spiritual Gifts in Body of Christ

texts.NIV84: But to each one of us grace has been given as Christ apportioned it.
texts.KJV: But unto every one of us is given grace according to the measure of the gift of Christ.
texts.NASB95: But to each one of us grace was given according to the measure of Christ’s gift.
texts.NKJV: But to each one of us grace was given according to the measure of Christ’s gift.
texts.DRB: But to every one of us is given grace, according to the measure of the giving of Christ.

texts.NIV84: This is why it says: "When he ascended on high, he led captives in his train and gave gifts to men."
texts.KJV: Wherefore he saith, When he ascended up on high, he led captivity captive, and gave gifts unto men.
texts.NASB95:
Therefore it says,
“When He ascended on high,
He led captive a host of captives,
And He gave gifts to men.”
texts.NKJV:
Therefore He says:
“When He ascended on high,
He led captivity captive,
And gave gifts to men.”
texts.DRB: Wherefore he saith: Ascending on high, he led captivity captive; he gave gifts to men.

texts.NIV84: (What does "he ascended" mean except that he also descended to the lower, earthly regions?
texts.KJV: (Now that he ascended, what is it but that he also descended first into the lower parts of the earth?
texts.NASB95: (Now this expression, “He ascended,” what does it mean except that He also had descended into the lower parts of the earth?
texts.NKJV: (Now this, “He ascended”—what does it mean but that He also first descended into the lower parts of the earth?
texts.DRB: Now that he ascended, what is it, but because he also descended first into the lower parts of the earth?

texts.NIV84: He who descended is the very one who ascended higher than all the heavens, in order to fill the whole universe.)
texts.KJV: He that descended is the same also that ascended up far above all heavens, that he might fill all things.)
texts.NASB95: He who descended is Himself also He who ascended far above all the heavens, so that He might fill all things.)
texts.NKJV: He who descended is also the One who ascended far above all the heavens, that He might fill all things.)
texts.DRB: He that descended is the same also that ascended above all the heavens, that he might fill all things.

texts.NIV84: It was he who gave some to be apostles, some to be prophets, some to be evangelists, and some to be pastors and teachers,
texts.KJV: And he gave some, apostles; and some, prophets; and some, evangelists; and some, pastors and teachers;
texts.NASB95: And He gave some as apostles, and some as prophets, and some as evangelists, and some as pastors and teachers,
texts.NKJV: And He Himself gave some to be apostles, some prophets, some evangelists, and some pastors and teachers,
texts.DRB: And he gave some apostles, and some prophets, and other some evangelists, and other some pastors and doctors,

texts.NIV84: to prepare God's people for works of service, so that the body of Christ may be built up
texts.KJV: For the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ:
texts.NASB95: for the equipping of the saints for the work of service, to the building up of the body of Christ;
texts.NKJV: for the equipping of the saints for the work of ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ,
texts.DRB: For the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ:

texts.NIV84: until we all reach unity in the faith and in the knowledge of the Son of God and become mature, attaining to the whole measure of the fullness of Christ.
texts.KJV: Till we all come in the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, unto a perfect man, unto the measure of the stature of the fulness of Christ:
texts.NASB95: until we all attain to the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, to a mature man, to the measure of the stature which belongs to the fullness of Christ.
texts.NKJV: till we all come to the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God, to a perfect man, to the measure of the stature of the fullness of Christ;

texts.NIV84: Then we will no longer be infants, tossed back and forth by the waves, and blown here and there by every wind of teaching and by the cunning and craftiness of men in their deceitful scheming.
texts.KJV: That we henceforth be no more children, tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the sleight of men, and cunning craftiness, whereby they lie in wait to deceive;
texts.NASB95: As a result, we are no longer to be children, tossed here and there by waves and carried about by every wind of doctrine, by the trickery of men, by craftiness in deceitful scheming;
texts.NKJV: that we should no longer be children, tossed to and fro and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the trickery of men, in the cunning craftiness of deceitful plotting,
texts.DRB: That henceforth we be no more children tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of doctrine by the wickedness of men, by cunning craftiness, by which they lie in wait to deceive

texts.NIV84: Instead, speaking the truth in love, we will in all things grow up into him who is the Head, that is, Christ.

texts.NASB95: but speaking the truth in love, we are to grow up in all aspects into Him who is the head, even Christ,
texts.NKJV: but, speaking the truth in love, may grow up in all things into Him who is the head—Christ—
texts.DRB: But doing the truth in charity, we may in all things grow up in him who is the head, even Christ:

texts.NIV84: From him the whole body, joined and held together by every supporting ligament, grows and builds itself up in love, as each part does its work.

texts.NASB95: from whom the whole body, being fitted and held together by what every joint supplies, according to the proper working of each individual part, causes the growth of the body for the building up of itself in love.
texts.NKJV: from whom the whole body, joined and knit together by what every joint supplies, according to the effective working by which every part does its share, causes growth of the body for the edifying of itself in love.
texts.DRB: From whom the whole body, being compacted and fitly joined together, by what every joint supplieth, according to the operation in the measure of every part, maketh increase of the body, unto the edifying of itself in charity.

texts.NASB95: So this I say, and affirm together with the Lord, that you walk no longer just as the Gentiles also walk, in the futility of their mind,
texts.NKJV: This I say, therefore, and testify in the Lord, that you should no longer walk as the rest of the Gentiles walk, in the futility of their mind,
texts.DRB: This then I say and testify in the Lord: That henceforward you walk not as also the Gentiles walk in the vanity of their mind,

texts.NIV84: They are darkened in their understanding and separated from the life of God because of the ignorance that is in them due to the hardening of their hearts.
texts.KJV: Having the understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God through the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart:

texts.NKJV: having their understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God, because of the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart;
texts.DRB: Having their understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God through the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their hearts.

texts.NIV84: Having lost all sensitivity, they have given themselves over to sensuality so as to indulge in every kind of impurity, with a continual lust for more.

texts.NASB95: and they, having become callous, have given themselves over to sensuality for the practice of every kind of impurity with greediness.

texts.DRB: Who despairing, have given themselves up to lasciviousness, unto the working of all uncleanness, unto the working of all uncleanness, unto covetousness.

texts.NASB95: But you did not learn Christ in this way,
texts.NKJV: But you have not so learned Christ,
texts.DRB: But you have not so learned Christ;

texts.NASB95: if indeed you have heard Him and have been taught in Him, just as truth is in Jesus,
texts.NKJV: if indeed you have heard Him and have been taught by Him, as the truth is in Jesus:
texts.DRB: If so be that you have heard him, and have been taught in him, as the truth is in Jesus:

texts.NASB95: that, in reference to your former manner of life, you lay aside the old self, which is being corrupted in accordance with the lusts of deceit,
texts.NKJV: that you put off, concerning your former conduct, the old man which grows corrupt according to the deceitful lusts,
texts.DRB: To put off, according to former conversation, the old man, who is corrupted according to the desire of error.

texts.NIV84: to be made new in the attitude of your minds;
texts.KJV: And be renewed in the spirit of your mind;
texts.NASB95: and that you be renewed in the spirit of your mind,
texts.NKJV: and be renewed in the spirit of your mind,
texts.DRB: And be renewed in the spirit of your mind:

texts.NIV84: and to put on the new self, created to be like God in true righteousness and holiness.
texts.KJV: And that ye put on the new man, which after God is created in righteousness and true holiness.
texts.NASB95: and put on the new self, which in the likeness of God has been created in righteousness and holiness of the truth.
texts.NKJV: and that you put on the new man which was created according to God, in true righteousness and holiness.
texts.DRB: And put on the new man, who according to God is created in justice and holiness of truth.

texts.NIV84: Therefore each of you must put off falsehood and speak truthfully to his neighbor, for we are all members of one body.
texts.KJV: Wherefore putting away lying, speak every man truth with his neighbour: for we are members one of another.
texts.NASB95: Therefore, laying aside falsehood, speak truth each one of you with his neighbor, for we are members of one another.
texts.NKJV: Therefore, putting away lying, “Let each one of you speak truth with his neighbor,” for we are members of one another.
texts.DRB: Wherefore putting away lying, speak ;ye the truth every man with his neighbour; for we are members one of another.

texts.NIV84: "In your anger do not sin": Do not let the sun go down while you are still angry,
texts.KJV: Be ye angry, and sin not: let not the sun go down upon your wrath:
texts.NASB95: Be angry, and yet do not sin; do not let the sun go down on your anger,
texts.NKJV: “Be angry, and do not sin”: do not let the sun go down on your wrath,
texts.DRB: Be angry, and sin not. Let not the sun go down upon your anger.

texts.NIV84: and do not give the devil a foothold.
texts.KJV: Neither give place to the devil.
texts.NASB95: and do not give the devil an opportunity.
texts.NKJV: nor give place to the devil.
texts.DRB: Give not place to the devil.

texts.NIV84: He who has been stealing must steal no longer, but must work, doing something useful with his own hands, that he may have something to share with those in need.
texts.KJV: Let him that stole steal no more: but rather let him labour, working with his hands the thing which is good, that he may have to give to him that needeth.
texts.NASB95: He who steals must steal no longer; but rather he must labor, performing with his own hands what is good, so that he will have something to share with one who has need.
texts.NKJV: Let him who stole steal no longer, but rather let him labor, working with his hands what is good, that he may have something to give him who has need.
texts.DRB: He that stole, let him now steal no more; but rather let him labour, working with his hands the thing which is good, that he may have something to give to him that suffereth need.

texts.NIV84: Do not let any unwholesome talk come out of your mouths, but only what is helpful for building others up according to their needs, that it may benefit those who listen.
texts.KJV: Let no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth, but that which is good to the use of edifying, that it may minister grace unto the hearers.
texts.NASB95: Let no unwholesome word proceed from your mouth, but only such a word as is good for edification according to the need of the moment, so that it will give grace to those who hear.
texts.NKJV: Let no corrupt word proceed out of your mouth, but what is good for necessary edification, that it may impart grace to the hearers.
texts.DRB: Let no evil speech proceed from your mouth; but that which is good, to the edification of faith, that it may administer grace to the hearers.

texts.NIV84: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, with whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
texts.KJV: And grieve not the holy Spirit of God, whereby ye are sealed unto the day of redemption.
texts.NASB95: Do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
texts.NKJV: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
texts.DRB: And grieve not the holy Spirit of God: whereby you are sealed unto the day of redemption.

texts.NIV84: Get rid of all bitterness, rage and anger, brawling and slander, along with every form of malice.
texts.KJV: Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice:
texts.NASB95: Let all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamor and slander be put away from you, along with all malice.
texts.NKJV: Let all bitterness, wrath, anger, clamor, and evil speaking be put away from you, with all malice.
texts.DRB: Let all bitterness, and anger, and indignation, and clamour, and blasphemy, be put away from you, with all malice.

texts.NIV84: Be kind and compassionate to one another, forgiving each other, just as in Christ God forgave you.
texts.KJV: And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ's sake hath forgiven you.
texts.NASB95: Be kind to one another, tender-hearted, forgiving each other, just as God in Christ also has forgiven you.
texts.NKJV: And be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God in Christ forgave you.
texts.DRB: And be ye kind one to another; merciful, forgiving one another, even as God hath forgiven you in Christ.

texts.NIV84: Wives, submit to your husbands as to the Lord.
texts.KJV: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as unto the Lord.
texts.NASB95: Wives, be subject to your own husbands, as to the Lord.
texts.NKJV: Wives, submit to your own husbands, as to the Lord.
texts.DRB: Let women be subject to their husbands, as to the Lord:

texts.NIV84: For the husband is the head of the wife as Christ is the head of the church, his body, of which he is the Savior.
texts.KJV: For the husband is the head of the wife, even as Christ is the head of the church: and he is the saviour of the body.
texts.NASB95: For the husband is the head of the wife, as Christ also is the head of the church, He Himself being the Savior of the body.
texts.NKJV: For the husband is head of the wife, as also Christ is head of the church; and He is the Savior of the body.
texts.DRB: Because the husband is the head of the wife, as Christ is the head of the church. He is the saviour of his body.

texts.NIV84: Now as the church submits to Christ, so also wives should submit to their husbands in everything.
texts.KJV: Therefore as the church is subject unto Christ, so let the wives be to their own husbands in every thing.
texts.NASB95: But as the church is subject to Christ, so also the wives ought to be to their husbands in everything.
texts.NKJV: Therefore, just as the church is subject to Christ, so let the wives be to their own husbands in everything.
texts.DRB: Therefore as the church is subject to Christ, so also let the wives be to their husbands in all things.

texts.NIV84: Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her
texts.KJV: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;
texts.NASB95: Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ also loved the church and gave Himself up for her,
texts.NKJV: Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ also loved the church and gave Himself for her,
texts.DRB: Husbands, love your wives, as Christ also loved the church, and delivered himself up for it:

texts.NIV84: to make her holy, cleansing her by the washing with water through the word,
texts.KJV: That he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word,
texts.NASB95: so that He might sanctify her, having cleansed her by the washing of water with the word,
texts.NKJV: that He might sanctify and cleanse her with the washing of water by the word,
texts.DRB: That he might sanctify it, cleansing it by the laver of water in the word of life:

texts.NIV84: and to present her to himself as a radiant church, without stain or wrinkle or any other blemish, but holy and blameless.
texts.KJV: That he might present it to himself a glorious church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without blemish.
texts.NASB95: that He might present to Himself the church in all her glory, having no spot or wrinkle or any such thing; but that she would be holy and blameless.
texts.NKJV: that He might present her to Himself a glorious church, not having spot or wrinkle or any such thing, but that she should be holy and without blemish.
texts.DRB: That he might present it to himself a glorious church, not having spot or wrinkle, or any; such thing; but that it should be holy, and without blemish.

texts.NIV84: In this same way, husbands ought to love their wives as their own bodies. He who loves his wife loves himself.
texts.KJV: So ought men to love their wives as their own bodies. He that loveth his wife loveth himself.
texts.NASB95: So husbands ought also to love their own wives as their own bodies. He who loves his own wife loves himself;
texts.NKJV: So husbands ought to love their own wives as their own bodies; he who loves his wife loves himself.
texts.DRB: So also ought men to love their wives as their own bodies. He that loveth his wife, loveth himself.

texts.NIV84: After all, no one ever hated his own body, but he feeds and cares for it, just as Christ does the church--
texts.KJV: For no man ever yet hated his own flesh; but nourisheth and cherisheth it, even as the Lord the church:
texts.NASB95: for no one ever hated his own flesh, but nourishes and cherishes it, just as Christ also does the church,
texts.NKJV: For no one ever hated his own flesh, but nourishes and cherishes it, just as the Lord does the church.
texts.DRB: For no man ever hated his own flesh; but nourisheth and cherisheth it, as also Christ doth the church:

texts.NIV84: for we are members of his body.
texts.KJV: For we are members of his body, of his flesh, and of his bones.
texts.NASB95: because we are members of His body.
texts.NKJV: For we are members of His body, of His flesh and of His bones.
texts.DRB: Because we are members of his body, of his flesh, and of his bones.

texts.NIV84: "For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife, and the two will become one flesh."
texts.KJV: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall be joined unto his wife, and they two shall be one flesh.
texts.NASB95: For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and shall be joined to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh.
texts.NKJV: “For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh.”
texts.DRB: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall cleave to his wife, and they shall be two in one flesh.

texts.NIV84: This is a profound mystery--but I am talking about Christ and the church.
texts.KJV: This is a great mystery: but I speak concerning Christ and the church.
texts.NASB95: This mystery is great; but I am speaking with reference to Christ and the church.
texts.NKJV: This is a great mystery, but I speak concerning Christ and the church.
texts.DRB: This is a great sacrament; but I speak in Christ and in the church.

texts.NIV84: However, each one of you also must love his wife as he loves himself, and the wife must respect her husband.
texts.KJV: Nevertheless let every one of you in particular so love his wife even as himself; and the wife see that she reverence her husband.
texts.NASB95: Nevertheless, each individual among you also is to love his own wife even as himself, and the wife must see to it that she respects her husband.
texts.NKJV: Nevertheless let each one of you in particular so love his own wife as himself, and let the wife see that she respects her husband.
texts.DRB: Nevertheless let every one of you in particular love his wife as himself: and let the wife fear her husband.

blocks.title: The Church is the Body of Christ

texts.NIV84: For this reason, ever since I heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all the saints,
texts.KJV: Wherefore I also, after I heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus, and love unto all the saints,
texts.NASB95: For this reason I too, having heard of the faith in the Lord Jesus which exists among you and your love for all the saints,
texts.NKJV: Therefore I also, after I heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all the saints,
texts.DRB: Wherefore I also, hearing of your faith that is in the Lord Jesus, and of your love towards all the saints,

texts.NIV84: I have not stopped giving thanks for you, remembering you in my prayers.
texts.KJV: Cease not to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers;
texts.NASB95: do not cease giving thanks for you, while making mention of you in my prayers;
texts.NKJV: do not cease to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers:
texts.DRB: Cease not to give thanks for you, making commemoration of you in my prayers,

texts.NIV84: I keep asking that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the glorious Father, may give you the Spirit of wisdom and revelation, so that you may know him better.
texts.KJV: That the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give unto you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him:
texts.NASB95: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give to you a spirit of wisdom and of revelation in the knowledge of Him.
texts.NKJV: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give to you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of Him,
texts.DRB: That the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give unto you the spirit of wisdom and of revelation, in the knowledge of him:

texts.NIV84: I pray also that the eyes of your heart may be enlightened in order that you may know the hope to which he has called you, the riches of his glorious inheritance in the saints,
texts.KJV: The eyes of your understanding being enlightened; that ye may know what is the hope of his calling, and what the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the saints,
texts.NASB95: I pray that the eyes of your heart may be enlightened, so that you will know what is the hope of His calling, what are the riches of the glory of His inheritance in the saints,
texts.NKJV: the eyes of your understanding being enlightened; that you may know what is the hope of His calling, what are the riches of the glory of His inheritance in the saints,
texts.DRB: The eyes of your heart enlightened, that you may know what the hope is of the glory of his inheritance in the saints.

texts.NIV84: and his incomparably great power for us who believe. That power is like the working of his mighty strength,
texts.KJV: And what is the exceeding greatness of his power to us-ward who believe, according to the working of his mighty power,
texts.NASB95: and what is the surpassing greatness of His power toward us who believe. These are in accordance with the working of the strength of His might
texts.NKJV: and what is the exceeding greatness of His power toward us who believe, according to the working of His mighty power
texts.DRB: And what is the exceeding greatness of his power towards us, who believe according to the operation of the might of his power,

texts.NIV84: which he exerted in Christ when he raised him from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly realms,
texts.KJV: Which he wrought in Christ, when he raised him from the dead, and set him at his own right hand in the heavenly places,
texts.NASB95: which He brought about in Christ, when He raised Him from the dead and seated Him at His right hand in the heavenly places,
texts.NKJV: which He worked in Christ when He raised Him from the dead and seated Him at His right hand in the heavenly places,
texts.DRB: Which he wrought in Christ, raising him up from the dead, and setting him on his right hand in the heavenly places.

texts.NIV84: far above all rule and authority, power and dominion, and every title that can be given, not only in the present age but also in the one to come.
texts.KJV: Far above all principality, and power, and might, and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this world, but also in that which is to come:
texts.NASB95: far above all rule and authority and power and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this age but also in the one to come.
texts.NKJV: far above all principality and power and might and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this age but also in that which is to come.
texts.DRB: Above all principality, and power, and virtue, and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this world, but also in that which is to come.

texts.NIV84: And God placed all things under his feet and appointed him to be head over everything for the church,
texts.KJV: And hath put all things under his feet, and gave him to be the head over all things to the church,
texts.NASB95: And He put all things in subjection under His feet, and gave Him as head over all things to the church,
texts.NKJV: And He put all things under His feet, and gave Him to be head over all things to the church,
texts.DRB: And he hath subjected all things under his feet, and hath made him head over all the church,

texts.NIV84: which is his body, the fullness of him who fills everything in every way.
texts.KJV: Which is his body, the fulness of him that filleth all in all.
texts.NASB95: which is His body, the fullness of Him who fills all in all.
texts.NKJV: which is His body, the fullness of Him who fills all in all.
texts.DRB: Which is his body, and the fulness of him who is filled all in all.

texts.NIV84: Now, brothers, about times and dates we do not need to write to you,
texts.KJV: But of the times and the seasons, brethren, ye have no need that I write unto you.
texts.NASB95: Now as to the times and the epochs, brethren, you have no need of anything to be written to you.
texts.NKJV: But concerning the times and the seasons, brethren, you have no need that I should write to you.
texts.DRB: But of the times and moments, brethren, you need not, that we should write to you;

texts.NIV84: for you know very well that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.
texts.KJV: For yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night.
texts.NASB95: For you yourselves know full well that the day of the Lord will come just like a thief in the night.
texts.NKJV: For you yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so comes as a thief in the night.
texts.DRB: For yourselves know perfectly, that the day of the Lord shall so come, as a thief in the night.

texts.NIV84: While people are saying, "Peace and safety," destruction will come on them suddenly, as labor pains on a pregnant woman, and they will not escape.
texts.KJV: For when they shall say, Peace and safety; then sudden destruction cometh upon them, as travail upon a woman with child; and they shall not escape.
texts.NASB95: While they are saying, “Peace and safety!” then destruction will come upon them suddenly like labor pains upon a woman with child, and they will not escape.
texts.NKJV: For when they say, “Peace and safety!” then sudden destruction comes upon them, as labor pains upon a pregnant woman. And they shall not escape.
texts.DRB: For when they shall say, peace and security; then shall sudden destruction come upon them, as the pains upon her that is with child, and they shall not escape.

texts.NIV84: But you, brothers, are not in darkness so that this day should surprise you like a thief.
texts.KJV: But ye, brethren, are not in darkness, that that day should overtake you as a thief.
texts.NASB95: But you, brethren, are not in darkness, that the day would overtake you like a thief;
texts.NKJV: But you, brethren, are not in darkness, so that this Day should overtake you as a thief.
texts.DRB: But you, brethren, are not in darkness, that that day should overtake you as a thief.

texts.NIV84: You are all sons of the light and sons of the day. We do not belong to the night or to the darkness.
texts.KJV: Ye are all the children of light, and the children of the day: we are not of the night, nor of darkness.
texts.NASB95: for you are all sons of light and sons of day. We are not of night nor of darkness;
texts.NKJV: You are all sons of light and sons of the day. We are not of the night nor of darkness.
texts.DRB: For all you are the children of light, and children of the day: we are not of the night, nor of darkness.

texts.NIV84: So then, let us not be like others, who are asleep, but let us be alert and self-controlled.
texts.KJV: Therefore let us not sleep, as do others; but let us watch and be sober.
texts.NASB95: so then let us not sleep as others do, but let us be alert and sober.
texts.NKJV: Therefore let us not sleep, as others do, but let us watch and be sober.
texts.DRB: Therefore, let us not sleep, as others do; but let us watch, and be sober.

texts.NIV84: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who get drunk, get drunk at night.
texts.KJV: For they that sleep sleep in the night; and they that be drunken are drunken in the night.
texts.NASB95: For those who sleep do their sleeping at night, and those who get drunk get drunk at night.
texts.NKJV: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who get drunk are drunk at night.
texts.DRB: For they that sleep, sleep in the night; and they that are drunk, are drunk in the night.

texts.NIV84: But since we belong to the day, let us be self-controlled, putting on faith and love as a breastplate, and the hope of salvation as a helmet.
texts.KJV: But let us, who are of the day, be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love; and for an helmet, the hope of salvation.
texts.NASB95: But since we are of the day, let us be sober, having put on the breastplate of faith and love, and as a helmet, the hope of salvation.
texts.NKJV: But let us who are of the day be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love, and as a helmet the hope of salvation.
texts.DRB: But let us, who are of the day, be sober, having on the breastplate of faith and charity, and for a helmet the hope of salvation.

texts.NIV84: For God did not appoint us to suffer wrath but to receive salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.KJV: For God hath not appointed us to wrath, but to obtain salvation by our Lord Jesus Christ,
texts.NASB95: For God has not destined us for wrath, but for obtaining salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
texts.NKJV: For God did not appoint us to wrath, but to obtain salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
texts.DRB: For God hath not appointed us unto wrath, but unto the purchasing of salvation by our Lord Jesus Christ,

texts.NIV84: He died for us so that, whether we are awake or asleep, we may live together with him.
texts.KJV: Who died for us, that, whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with him.
texts.NASB95: who died for us, so that whether we are awake or asleep, we will live together with Him.
texts.NKJV: who died for us, that whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with Him.
texts.DRB: Who died for us; that, whether we watch or sleep, we may live together with him.

texts.NIV84: Therefore encourage one another and build each other up, just as in fact you are doing.
texts.KJV: Wherefore comfort yourselves together, and edify one another, even as also ye do.
texts.NASB95: Therefore encourage one another and build up one another, just as you also are doing.
texts.NKJV: Therefore comfort each other and edify one another, just as you also are doing.
texts.DRB: For which cause comfort one another; and edify one another, as you also do.

texts.NIV84: Now we ask you, brothers, to respect those who work hard among you, who are over you in the Lord and who admonish you.
texts.KJV: And we beseech you, brethren, to know them which labour among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you;
texts.NASB95: But we request of you, brethren, that you appreciate those who diligently labor among you, and have charge over you in the Lord and give you instruction,
texts.NKJV: And we urge you, brethren, to recognize those who labor among you, and are over you in the Lord and admonish you,
texts.DRB: And we beseech you, brethren, to know them who labour among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you:

texts.NIV84: Hold them in the highest regard in love because of their work. Live in peace with each other.
texts.KJV: And to esteem them very highly in love for their work's sake. And be at peace among yourselves.
texts.NASB95: and that you esteem them very highly in love because of their work. Live in peace with one another.
texts.NKJV: and to esteem them very highly in love for their work’s sake. Be at peace among yourselves.
texts.DRB: That you esteem them more abundantly in charity, for their work's sake. Have peace with them.

texts.NIV84: And we urge you, brothers, warn those who are idle, encourage the timid, help the weak, be patient with everyone.
texts.KJV: Now we exhort you, brethren, warn them that are unruly, comfort the feebleminded, support the weak, be patient toward all men.
texts.NASB95: We urge you, brethren, admonish the unruly, encourage the fainthearted, help the weak, be patient with everyone.
texts.NKJV: Now we exhort you, brethren, warn those who are unruly, comfort the fainthearted, uphold the weak, be patient with all.
texts.DRB: And we beseech you, brethren, rebuke the unquiet, comfort the feeble minded, support the weak, be patient towards all men.

texts.NIV84: Make sure that nobody pays back wrong for wrong, but always try to be kind to each other and to everyone else.
texts.KJV: See that none render evil for evil unto any man; but ever follow that which is good, both among yourselves, and to all men.
texts.NASB95: See that no one repays another with evil for evil, but always seek after that which is good for one another and for all people.
texts.NKJV: See that no one renders evil for evil to anyone, but always pursue what is good both for yourselves and for all.
texts.DRB: See that none render evil for evil to any man; but ever follow that which is good towards each other, and towards all men.

texts.NIV84: Be joyful always;
texts.KJV: Rejoice evermore.
texts.NASB95: Rejoice always;
texts.NKJV: Rejoice always,
texts.DRB: Always rejoice.

texts.NIV84: pray continually;
texts.KJV: Pray without ceasing.
texts.NASB95: pray without ceasing;
texts.NKJV: pray without ceasing,
texts.DRB: Pray without ceasing.

texts.NIV84: give thanks in all circumstances, for this is God's will for you in Christ Jesus.
texts.KJV: In every thing give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you.
texts.NASB95: in everything give thanks; for this is God’s will for you in Christ Jesus.
texts.NKJV: in everything give thanks; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.
texts.DRB: In all things give thanks; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you all.

texts.NIV84: Do not put out the Spirit's fire;
texts.KJV: Quench not the Spirit.
texts.NASB95: Do not quench the Spirit;
texts.NKJV: Do not quench the Spirit.
texts.DRB: Extinguish not the spirit.

texts.NIV84: do not treat prophecies with contempt.
texts.KJV: Despise not prophesyings.
texts.NASB95: do not despise prophetic utterances.
texts.NKJV: Do not despise prophecies.
texts.DRB: Despise not prophecies.

texts.NIV84: Test everything. Hold on to the good.
texts.KJV: Prove all things; hold fast that which is good.
texts.NASB95: But examine everything carefully; hold fast to that which is good;
texts.NKJV: Test all things; hold fast what is good.
texts.DRB: But prove all things; hold fast that which is good.

texts.NIV84: Avoid every kind of evil.
texts.KJV: Abstain from all appearance of evil.
texts.NASB95: abstain from every form of evil.
texts.NKJV: Abstain from every form of evil.
texts.DRB: From all appearance of evil refrain yourselves.

texts.NIV84: May God himself, the God of peace, sanctify you through and through. May your whole spirit, soul and body be kept blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.KJV: And the very God of peace sanctify you wholly; and I pray God your whole spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.NASB95: Now may the God of peace Himself sanctify you entirely; and may your spirit and soul and body be preserved complete, without blame at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.NKJV: Now may the God of peace Himself sanctify you completely; and may your whole spirit, soul, and body be preserved blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
texts.DRB: And may the God of peace himself sanctify you in all things; that your whole spirit, and soul, and body, may be preserved blameless in the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.

texts.NIV84: The one who calls you is faithful and he will do it.
texts.KJV: Faithful is he that calleth you, who also will do it.
texts.NASB95: Faithful is He who calls you, and He also will bring it to pass.
texts.NKJV: He who calls you is faithful, who also will do it.
texts.DRB: He is faithful who hath called you, who also will do it.

texts.NIV84: Brothers, pray for us.
texts.KJV: Brethren, pray for us.
texts.NASB95: Brethren, pray for us.
texts.NKJV: Brethren, pray for us.
texts.DRB: Brethren, pray for us.

texts.NIV84: Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.
texts.KJV: Greet all the brethren with an holy kiss.
texts.NASB95: Greet all the brethren with a holy kiss.
texts.NKJV: Greet all the brethren with a holy kiss.
texts.DRB: Salute all the brethren with a holy kiss.

texts.NIV84: I charge you before the Lord to have this letter read to all the brothers.
texts.KJV: I charge you by the Lord that this epistle be read unto all the holy brethren.
texts.NASB95: I adjure you by the Lord to have this letter read to all the brethren.
texts.NKJV: I charge you by the Lord that this epistle be read to all the holy brethren.
texts.DRB: I charge you by the Lord, that this epistle be read to all the holy brethren.

texts.NIV84: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
texts.KJV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
texts.NASB95: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
texts.NKJV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
texts.DRB: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.

blocks.title: Idol Feasts and the Lord\u2019s Supper

texts.NIV84: Therefore, my dear friends, flee from idolatry.
texts.KJV: Wherefore, my dearly beloved, flee from idolatry.
texts.NASB95: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
texts.NKJV: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
texts.DRB: Wherefore, my dearly beloved, fly from the service of idols.

texts.NIV84: I speak to sensible people; judge for yourselves what I say.
texts.KJV: I speak as to wise men; judge ye what I say.
texts.NASB95: I speak as to wise men; you judge what I say.
texts.NKJV: I speak as to wise men; judge for yourselves what I say.
texts.DRB: I speak as to wise men: judge ye yourselves what I say.

texts.NIV84: Is not the cup of thanksgiving for which we give thanks a participation in the blood of Christ? And is not the bread that we break a participation in the body of Christ?
texts.KJV: The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?
texts.NASB95: Is not the cup of blessing which we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Is not the bread which we break a sharing in the body of Christ?
texts.NKJV: The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?
texts.DRB: The chalice of benediction, which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ ? And the bread, which we break, is it not the partaking of the body of the Lord ?

texts.NIV84: Because there is one loaf, we, who are many, are one body, for we all partake of the one loaf.
texts.KJV: For we being many are one bread, and one body: for we are all partakers of that one bread.
texts.NASB95: Since there is one bread, we who are many are one body; for we all partake of the one bread.
texts.NKJV: For we, though many, are one bread and one body; for we all partake of that one bread.
texts.DRB: For we, being many, are one bread, one body, all that partake of one bread.

texts.NIV84: Consider the people of Israel: Do not those who eat the sacrifices participate in the altar?
texts.KJV: Behold Israel after the flesh: are not they which eat of the sacrifices partakers of the altar?
texts.NASB95: Look at the nation Israel; are not those who eat the sacrifices sharers in the altar?
texts.NKJV: Observe Israel after the flesh: Are not those who eat of the sacrifices partakers of the altar?
texts.DRB: Behold Israel according to the flesh: are not they, that eat of the sacrifices, partakers of the altar ?

texts.NIV84: Do I mean then that a sacrifice offered to an idol is anything, or that an idol is anything?
texts.KJV: What say I then? that the idol is any thing, or that which is offered in sacrifice to idols is any thing?
texts.NASB95: What do I mean then? That a thing sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
texts.NKJV: What am I saying then? That an idol is anything, or what is offered to idols is anything?
texts.DRB: What then ? Do I say, that what is offered in sacrifice to idols, is any thing ? Or, that the idol is any thing ?

texts.NIV84: No, but the sacrifices of pagans are offered to demons, not to God, and I do not want you to be participants with demons.
texts.KJV: But I say, that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to devils, and not to God: and I would not that ye should have fellowship with devils.
texts.NASB95: No, but I say that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons and not to God; and I do not want you to become sharers in demons.
texts.NKJV: Rather, that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice they sacrifice to demons and not to God, and I do not want you to have fellowship with demons.
texts.DRB: But the things which the heathens sacrifice, they sacrifice to devils, and not to God. And I would not that you should be made partakers with devils.

texts.NIV84: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons too; you cannot have a part in both the Lord's table and the table of demons.
texts.KJV: Ye cannot drink the cup of the Lord, and the cup of devils: ye cannot be partakers of the Lord's table, and of the table of devils.
texts.NASB95: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
texts.NKJV: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cannot partake of the Lord’s table and of the table of demons.
texts.DRB: You cannot drink the chalice of the Lord, and the chalice of devils: you cannot be partakers of the table of the Lord, and of the table of devils.

texts.NIV84: Are we trying to arouse the Lord's jealousy? Are we stronger than he?
texts.KJV: Do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? are we stronger than he?
texts.NASB95: Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? We are not stronger than He, are we?
texts.NKJV: Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than He?
texts.DRB: Do we provoke the Lord to jealousy ? Are we stronger than he? All things are lawful for me, but all things are not expedient.

texts.NIV84: In the following directives I have no praise for you, for your meetings do more harm than good.
texts.KJV: Now in this that I declare unto you I praise you not, that ye come together not for the better, but for the worse.
texts.NASB95: But in giving this instruction, I do not praise you, because you come together not for the better but for the worse.
texts.NKJV: Now in giving these instructions I do not praise you, since you come together not for the better but for the worse.
texts.DRB: Now this I ordain: not praising you, that you come together not for the better, but for the worse.

texts.NIV84: In the first place, I hear that when you come together as a church, there are divisions among you, and to some extent I believe it.
texts.KJV: For first of all, when ye come together in the church, I hear that there be divisions among you; and I partly believe it.
texts.NASB95: For, in the first place, when you come together as a church, I hear that divisions exist among you; and in part I believe it.
texts.NKJV: For first of all, when you come together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and in part I believe it.
texts.DRB: For first of all I hear that when you come together in the church, there are schisms among you; and in part I believe it.

texts.NIV84: No doubt there have to be differences among you to show which of you have God's approval.
texts.KJV: For there must be also heresies among you, that they which are approved may be made manifest among you.
texts.NASB95: For there must also be factions among you, so that those who are approved may become evident among you.
texts.NKJV: For there must also be factions among you, that those who are approved may be recognized among you.
texts.DRB: For there must be also heresies: that they also, who are approved, may be made manifest among you.

texts.NIV84: When you come together, it is not the Lord's Supper you eat,
texts.KJV: When ye come together therefore into one place, this is not to eat the Lord's supper.
texts.NASB95: Therefore when you meet together, it is not to eat the Lord’s Supper,
texts.NKJV: Therefore when you come together in one place, it is not to eat the Lord’s Supper.
texts.DRB: When you come therefore together into one place, it is not now to eat the Lord's supper.

texts.NIV84: for as you eat, each of you goes ahead without waiting for anybody else. One remains hungry, another gets drunk.
texts.KJV: For in eating every one taketh before other his own supper: and one is hungry, and another is drunken.
texts.NASB95: for in your eating each one takes his own supper first; and one is hungry and another is drunk.
texts.NKJV: For in eating, each one takes his own supper ahead of others; and one is hungry and another is drunk.
texts.DRB: For every one taketh before his own supper to eat. And one indeed is hungry and another is drunk.

texts.NIV84: Don't you have homes to eat and drink in? Or do you despise the church of God and humiliate those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you for this? Certainly not!
texts.KJV: What? have ye not houses to eat and to drink in? or despise ye the church of God, and shame them that have not? what shall I say to you? shall I praise you in this? I praise you not.
texts.NASB95: What! Do you not have houses in which to eat and drink? Or do you despise the church of God and shame those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you? In this I will not praise you.
texts.NKJV: What! Do you not have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you despise the church of God and shame those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you in this? I do not praise you.
texts.DRB: What, have you not houses to eat and to drink in? Or despise ye the church of God; and put them to shame that have not ? What shall I say to you? Do I praise you? In this I praise you not.

texts.NIV84: For I received from the Lord what I also passed on to you: The Lord Jesus, on the night he was betrayed, took bread,
texts.KJV: For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you, that the Lord Jesus the same night in which he was betrayed took bread:
texts.NASB95: For I received from the Lord that which I also delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus in the night in which He was betrayed took bread;
texts.NKJV: For I received from the Lord that which I also delivered to you: that the Lord Jesus on the same night in which He was betrayed took bread;
texts.DRB: For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you, that the Lord Jesus, the same night in which he was betrayed, took bread.

texts.NIV84: and when he had given thanks, he broke it and said, "This is my body, which is for you; do this in remembrance of me."
texts.KJV: And when he had given thanks, he brake it, and said, Take, eat: this is my body, which is broken for you: this do in remembrance of me.
texts.NASB95: and when He had given thanks, He broke it and said, “This is My body, which is for you; do this in remembrance of Me.”
texts.NKJV: and when He had given thanks, He broke it and said, “Take, eat; this is My body which is broken for you; do this in remembrance of Me.”
texts.DRB: And giving thanks, broke, and said: Take ye, and eat: this is my body, which shall be delivered for you: this do for the commemoration of me.

texts.NIV84: In the same way, after supper he took the cup, saying, "This cup is the new covenant in my blood; do this, whenever you drink it, in remembrance of me."
texts.KJV: After the same manner also he took the cup, when he had supped, saying, this cup is the new testament in my blood: this do ye, as oft as ye drink it, in remembrance of me.
texts.NASB95: In the same way He took the cup also after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in My blood; do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of Me.”
texts.NKJV: In the same manner He also took the cup after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in My blood. This do, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of Me.”
texts.DRB: In like manner also the chalice, after he had supped, saying: This chalice is the new testament in my blood: this do ye, as often as you shall drink, for the commemoration of me.

texts.NIV84: For whenever you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord's death until he comes.
texts.KJV: For as often as ye eat this bread, and drink this cup, ye do shew the Lord's death till he come.
texts.NASB95: For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until He comes.
texts.NKJV: For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death till He comes.
texts.DRB: For as often as you shall eat this bread, and drink the chalice, you shall shew the death of the Lord, until he come.

texts.NIV84: Therefore, whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty of sinning against the body and blood of the Lord.
texts.KJV: Wherefore whosoever shall eat this bread, and drink this cup of the Lord, unworthily, shall be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
texts.NASB95: Therefore whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner, shall be guilty of the body and the blood of the Lord.
texts.NKJV: Therefore whoever eats this bread or drinks this cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
texts.DRB: Therefore whosoever shall eat this bread, or drink the chalice of the Lord unworthily, shall be guilty of the body and of the blood of the Lord.

texts.NIV84: A man ought to examine himself before he eats of the bread and drinks of the cup.
texts.KJV: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of that bread, and drink of that cup.
texts.NASB95: But a man must examine himself, and in so doing he is to eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
texts.NKJV: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
texts.DRB: But let a man prove himself: and so let him eat of that bread, and drink of the chalice.

texts.NIV84: For anyone who eats and drinks without recognizing the body of the Lord eats and drinks judgment on himself.
texts.KJV: For he that eateth and drinketh unworthily, eateth and drinketh damnation to himself, not discerning the Lord's body.
texts.NASB95: For he who eats and drinks, eats and drinks judgment to himself if he does not judge the body rightly.
texts.NKJV: For he who eats and drinks in an unworthy manner eats and drinks judgment to himself, not discerning the Lord’s body.
texts.DRB: For he that eateth and drinketh unworthily, eateth and drinketh judgment to himself, not discerning the body of the Lord.

texts.NIV84: That is why many among you are weak and sick, and a number of you have fallen asleep.
texts.KJV: For this cause many are weak and sickly among you, and many sleep.
texts.NASB95: For this reason many among you are weak and sick, and a number sleep.
texts.NKJV: For this reason many are weak and sick among you, and many sleep.
texts.DRB: Therefore are there many inform and weak among you, and many sleep.

texts.NIV84: But if we judged ourselves, we would not come under judgment.
texts.KJV: For if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.
texts.NASB95: But if we judged ourselves rightly, we would not be judged.
texts.NKJV: For if we would judge ourselves, we would not be judged.
texts.DRB: But if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.

texts.NIV84: When we are judged by the Lord, we are being disciplined so that we will not be condemned with the world.
texts.KJV: But when we are judged, we are chastened of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world.
texts.NASB95: But when we are judged, we are disciplined by the Lord so that we will not be condemned along with the world.
texts.NKJV: But when we are judged, we are chastened by the Lord, that we may not be condemned with the world.
texts.DRB: But whilst we are judged, we are chastised by the Lord, that we be not condemned with this world.

texts.NIV84: So then, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for each other.
texts.KJV: Wherefore, my brethren, when ye come together to eat, tarry one for another.
texts.NASB95: So then, my brethren, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
texts.NKJV: Therefore, my brethren, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
texts.DRB: Wherefore, my brethren, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.

texts.NIV84: If anyone is hungry, he should eat at home, so that when you meet together it may not result in judgment. And when I come I will give further directions.
texts.KJV: And if any man hunger, let him eat at home; that ye come not together unto condemnation. And the rest will I set in order when I come.
texts.NASB95: If anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, so that you will not come together for judgment. The remaining matters I will arrange when I come.
texts.NKJV: But if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, lest you come together for judgment. And the rest I will set in order when I come.
texts.DRB: If any man be hungry, let him eat at home; that you come not together unto judgment. And the rest I will set in order, when I come.

blocks.title: Unity and Diversity in the Body

texts.NIV84: The body is a unit, though it is made up of many parts; and though all its parts are many, they form one body. So it is with Christ.
texts.KJV: For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of that one body, being many, are one body: so also is Christ.
texts.NASB95: For even as the body is one and yet has many members, and all the members of the body, though they are many, are one body, so also is Christ.
texts.NKJV: For as the body is one and has many members, but all the members of that one body, being many, are one body, so also is Christ.
texts.DRB: For as the body is one, and hath many members; and all the members of the body, whereas they are many, yet are one body, so also is Christ.

texts.NIV84: For we were all baptized by one Spirit into one body--whether Jews or Greeks, slave or free--and we were all given the one Spirit to drink.
texts.KJV: For by one Spirit are we all baptized into one body, whether we be Jews or Gentiles, whether we be bond or free; and have been all made to drink into one Spirit.
texts.NASB95: For by one Spirit we were all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, whether slaves or free, and we were all made to drink of one Spirit.
texts.NKJV: For by one Spirit we were all baptized into one body—whether Jews or Greeks, whether slaves or free—and have all been made to drink into one Spirit.
texts.DRB: For in one Spirit were we all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Gentiles, whether bond or free; and in one Spirit we have all been made to drink.

texts.NIV84: Now the body is not made up of one part but of many.
texts.KJV: For the body is not one member, but many.
texts.NASB95: For the body is not one member, but many.
texts.NKJV: For in fact the body is not one member but many.
texts.DRB: For the body also is not one member, but many.

texts.NIV84: If the foot should say, "Because I am not a hand, I do not belong to the body," it would not for that reason cease to be part of the body.
texts.KJV: If the foot shall say, Because I am not the hand, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
texts.NASB95: If the foot says, “Because I am not a hand, I am not a part of the body,” it is not for this reason any the less a part of the body.
texts.NKJV: If the foot should say, “Because I am not a hand, I am not of the body,” is it therefore not of the body?
texts.DRB: If the foot should say, because I am not the hand, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body ?

texts.NIV84: And if the ear should say, "Because I am not an eye, I do not belong to the body," it would not for that reason cease to be part of the body.
texts.KJV: And if the ear shall say, Because I am not the eye, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
texts.NASB95: And if the ear says, “Because I am not an eye, I am not a part of the body,” it is not for this reason any the less a part of the body.
texts.NKJV: And if the ear should say, “Because I am not an eye, I am not of the body,” is it therefore not of the body?
texts.DRB: And if the ear should say, because I am not the eye, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body ?

texts.NIV84: If the whole body were an eye, where would the sense of hearing be? If the whole body were an ear, where would the sense of smell be?
texts.KJV: If the whole body were an eye, where were the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where were the smelling?
texts.NASB95: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole were hearing, where would the sense of smell be?
texts.NKJV: If the whole body were an eye, where would be the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where would be the smelling?
texts.DRB: If the whole body were the eye, where would be the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where would be the smelling?

texts.NIV84: But in fact God has arranged the parts in the body, every one of them, just as he wanted them to be.
texts.KJV: But now hath God set the members every one of them in the body, as it hath pleased him.
texts.NASB95: But now God has placed the members, each one of them, in the body, just as He desired.
texts.NKJV: But now God has set the members, each one of them, in the body just as He pleased.
texts.DRB: But now God hath set the members every one of them in the body as it hath pleased him.

texts.NIV84: If they were all one part, where would the body be?
texts.KJV: And if they were all one member, where were the body?
texts.NASB95: If they were all one member, where would the body be?
texts.NKJV: And if they were all one member, where would the body be?
texts.DRB: And if they all were one member, where would be the body?

texts.NIV84: As it is, there are many parts, but one body.
texts.KJV: But now are they many members, yet but one body.
texts.NASB95: But now there are many members, but one body.
texts.NKJV: But now indeed there are many members, yet one body.
texts.DRB: But now there are many members indeed, yet one body.

texts.NIV84: The eye cannot say to the hand, "I don't need you!" And the head cannot say to the feet, "I don't need you!"
texts.KJV: And the eye cannot say unto the hand, I have no need of thee: nor again the head to the feet, I have no need of you.
texts.NASB95: And the eye cannot say to the hand, “I have no need of you”; or again the head to the feet, “I have no need of you.”
texts.NKJV: And the eye cannot say to the hand, “I have no need of you”; nor again the head to the feet, “I have no need of you.”
texts.DRB: And the eye cannot say to the hand: I need not thy help; nor again the head to the feet: I have no need of you.

texts.NIV84: On the contrary, those parts of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable,
texts.KJV: Nay, much more those members of the body, which seem to be more feeble, are necessary:
texts.NASB95: On the contrary, it is much truer that the members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary;
texts.NKJV: No, much rather, those members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary.
texts.DRB: Yea, much more those that seem to be the more feeble members of the body, are more necessary.

texts.NIV84: and the parts that we think are less honorable we treat with special honor. And the parts that are unpresentable are treated with special modesty,
texts.KJV: And those members of the body, which we think to be less honourable, upon these we bestow more abundant honour; and our uncomely parts have more abundant comeliness.
texts.NASB95: and those members of the body which we deem less honorable, on these we bestow more abundant honor, and our less presentable members become much more presentable,
texts.NKJV: And those members of the body which we think to be less honorable, on these we bestow greater honor; and our unpresentable parts have greater modesty,
texts.DRB: And such as we think to be the less honourable members of the body, about these we put more abundant honour; and those that are our uncomely parts, have more abundant comeliness.

texts.NIV84: while our presentable parts need no special treatment. But God has combined the members of the body and has given greater honor to the parts that lacked it,
texts.KJV: For our comely parts have no need: but God hath tempered the body together, having given more abundant honour to that part which lacked.
texts.NASB95: whereas our more presentable members have no need of it. But God has so composed the body, giving more abundant honor to that member which lacked,
texts.NKJV: but our presentable parts have no need. But God composed the body, having given greater honor to that part which lacks it,
texts.DRB: But our comely parts have no need: but God hath tempered the body together, giving to that which wanted the more abundant honour,

texts.NIV84: so that there should be no division in the body, but that its parts should have equal concern for each other.
texts.KJV: That there should be no schism in the body; but that the members should have the same care one for another.
texts.NASB95: so that there may be no division in the body, but that the members may have the same care for one another.
texts.NKJV: that there should be no schism in the body, but that the members should have the same care for one another.
texts.DRB: That there might be no schism in the body; but the members might be mutually careful one for another.

texts.NIV84: If one part suffers, every part suffers with it; if one part is honored, every part rejoices with it.
texts.KJV: And whether one member suffer, all the members suffer with it; or one member be honoured, all the members rejoice with it.
texts.NASB95: And if one member suffers, all the members suffer with it; if one member is honored, all the members rejoice with it.
texts.NKJV: And if one member suffers, all the members suffer with it; or if one member is honored, all the members rejoice with it.
texts.DRB: And if one member suffer any thing, all the members suffer with it; or if one member glory, all the members rejoice with it.

texts.NIV84: Now you are the body of Christ, and each one of you is a part of it.
texts.KJV: Now ye are the body of Christ, and members in particular.
texts.NASB95: Now you are Christ’s body, and individually members of it.
texts.NKJV: Now you are the body of Christ, and members individually.
texts.DRB: Now you are the body of Christ, and members of member.

texts.NIV84: And in the church God has appointed first of all apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then workers of miracles, also those having gifts of healing, those able to help others, those with gifts of administration, and those speaking in different kinds of tongues.
texts.KJV: And God hath set some in the church, first apostles, secondarily prophets, thirdly teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, diversities of tongues.
texts.NASB95: And God has appointed in the church, first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, administrations, various kinds of tongues.
texts.NKJV: And God has appointed these in the church: first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, administrations, varieties of tongues.
texts.DRB: And God indeed hath set some in the church; first apostles, secondly prophets, thirdly doctors; after that miracles; then the graces of healing, helps, governments, kinds of tongues, interpretations of speeches.

texts.NIV84: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work miracles?
texts.KJV: Are all apostles? are all prophets? are all teachers? are all workers of miracles?
texts.NASB95: All are not apostles, are they? All are not prophets, are they? All are not teachers, are they? All are not workers of miracles, are they?
texts.NKJV: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Are all workers of miracles?
texts.DRB: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all doctors?

texts.NIV84: Do all have gifts of healing? Do all speak in tongues? Do all interpret?
texts.KJV: Have all the gifts of healing? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?
texts.NASB95: All do not have gifts of healings, do they? All do not speak with tongues, do they? All do not interpret, do they?
texts.NKJV: Do all have gifts of healings? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?
texts.DRB: Are all workers of miracles? Have all the grace of healing? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?

texts.NIV84: But eagerly desire the greater gifts. And now I will show you the most excellent way.
texts.KJV: But covet earnestly the best gifts: and yet shew I unto you a more excellent way.
texts.NASB95:
But earnestly desire the greater gifts.
And I show you a still more excellent way.
texts.NKJV: But earnestly desire the best gifts. And yet I show you a more excellent way.
texts.DRB: But be zealous for the better gifts. And I shew unto you yet a more excellent way.

texts.NIV84: Now we know that if the earthly tent we live in is destroyed, we have a building from God, an eternal house in heaven, not built by human hands.
texts.KJV: For we know that if our earthly house of this tabernacle were dissolved, we have a building of God, an house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
texts.NASB95: For we know that if the earthly tent which is our house is torn down, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
texts.NKJV: For we know that if our earthly house, this tent, is destroyed, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
texts.DRB: For we know, if our earthly house of this habitation be dissolved, that we have a building of God, a house not made with hands, eternal in heaven.

texts.NIV84: Meanwhile we groan, longing to be clothed with our heavenly dwelling,
texts.KJV: For in this we groan, earnestly desiring to be clothed upon with our house which is from heaven:
texts.NASB95: For indeed in this house we groan, longing to be clothed with our dwelling from heaven,
texts.NKJV: For in this we groan, earnestly desiring to be clothed with our habitation which is from heaven,
texts.DRB: For in this also we groan, desiring to be clothed upon with our habitation that is from heaven.

texts.NIV84: because when we are clothed, we will not be found naked.
texts.KJV: If so be that being clothed we shall not be found naked.
texts.NASB95: inasmuch as we, having put it on, will not be found naked.
texts.NKJV: if indeed, having been clothed, we shall not be found naked.
texts.DRB: Yet so that we be found clothed, not naked.

texts.NIV84: For while we are in this tent, we groan and are burdened, because we do not wish to be unclothed but to be clothed with our heavenly dwelling, so that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life.
texts.KJV: For we that are in this tabernacle do groan, being burdened: not for that we would be unclothed, but clothed upon, that mortality might be swallowed up of life.
texts.NASB95: For indeed while we are in this tent, we groan, being burdened, because we do not want to be unclothed but to be clothed, so that what is mortal will be swallowed up by life.
texts.NKJV: For we who are in this tent groan, being burdened, not because we want to be unclothed, but further clothed, that mortality may be swallowed up by life.
texts.DRB: For we also, who are in this tabernacle, do groan, being burthened; because we would not be unclothed, but clothed upon, that that which is mortal may be swallowed up by life.

texts.NIV84: Now it is God who has made us for this very purpose and has given us the Spirit as a deposit, guaranteeing what is to come.
texts.KJV: Now he that hath wrought us for the selfsame thing is God, who also hath given unto us the earnest of the Spirit.
texts.NASB95: Now He who prepared us for this very purpose is God, who gave to us the Spirit as a pledge.
texts.NKJV: Now He who has prepared us for this very thing is God, who also has given us the Spirit as a guarantee.
texts.DRB: Now he that maketh us for this very thing, is God, who hath given us the pledge of the Spirit.

texts.NIV84: Therefore we are always confident and know that as long as we are at home in the body we are away from the Lord.
texts.KJV: Therefore we are always confident, knowing that, whilst we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord:
texts.NASB95: Therefore, being always of good courage, and knowing that while we are at home in the body we are absent from the Lord—
texts.NKJV: So we are always confident, knowing that while we are at home in the body we are absent from the Lord.
texts.DRB: Therefore having always confidence, knowing that, while we are in the body, we are absent from the Lord.

texts.NIV84: We live by faith, not by sight.
texts.KJV: (For we walk by faith, not by sight:)
texts.NASB95: for we walk by faith, not by sight—
texts.NKJV: For we walk by faith, not by sight.
texts.DRB: (For we walk by faith, and not by sight.)

texts.NIV84: We are confident, I say, and would prefer to be away from the body and at home with the Lord.
texts.KJV: We are confident, I say, and willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.
texts.NASB95: we are of good courage, I say, and prefer rather to be absent from the body and to be at home with the Lord.
texts.NKJV: We are confident, yes, well pleased rather to be absent from the body and to be present with the Lord.
texts.DRB: But we are confident, and have a good will to be absent rather from the body, and to be present with the Lord.

texts.NIV84: So we make it our goal to please him, whether we are at home in the body or away from it.
texts.KJV: Wherefore we labour, that, whether present or absent, we may be accepted of him.
texts.NASB95: Therefore we also have as our ambition, whether at home or absent, to be pleasing to Him.
texts.NKJV: Therefore we make it our aim, whether present or absent, to be well pleasing to Him.
texts.DRB: And therefore we labour, whether absent or present, to please him.

texts.NIV84: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, that each one may receive what is due him for the things done while in the body, whether good or bad.
texts.KJV: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body, according to that he hath done, whether it be good or bad.
texts.NASB95: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, so that each one may be recompensed for his deeds in the body, according to what he has done, whether good or bad.
texts.NKJV: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, that each one may receive the things done in the body, according to what he has done, whether good or bad.
texts.DRB: For we must all be manifested before the judgement seat of Christ, that every one may receive the proper things of the body, according as he hath done, whether it be good or evil.
